### Chinese New Year Fireworks Harbour Cruise from Bauhinia Cruise

Unlike other harbour tours, the Harbour Cruise – Bauhinia is a large vessel which is able to accommodate over 400 passengers. Besides, it is a local company that owns cruise vessels with marine restaurant license. While watching fireworks, you can also enjoy the buffet dinner on board.

Bauhinia Cruise Night Harbour Tour Hong Kong [...] Program: 3-hour harbour tour with dinner buffet and free flow of selected drinks  
 Highlight:　Watch fireworks + sail along Victoria Harbour + light buffet + free flow of drink  
 Date:　February 18, 2026  
 Sail time:　Embark from Central Pier No.8 and return at 6:30pm and return at 9:30pm  
 Ticket:　HK$ HK$ 1,900 (with/without window seat, 2/F & 3/F viewing deck)  
 Ticket:　Start from HK$ HK$ 4,400 for two pax (with/without window seat, 4/F viewing deck)

### Fireworks Boat Tour with Watertours

Watertours Hong Kong Ltd. is a subsidiary of Wing On Travel and established in the late 1950s. Their tour is on a Chinese-style tour boat offering narrations in English (supplementary by Mandarin).

# Ultimate Guide to Watching the 2026 Chinese New Year Fireworks on a Victoria Harbour Tour

#### Share This Story to:

For the 2026 Hong Kong CNY fireworks, nothing beats **the magic of enjoying them on a harbour cruise and boat ride** on the harbour – it’s the premier choice for a panoramic, front-row and crowd-free view and relaxed experience.

With limited tickets now on sale from traditional **junk boat**, **private yacht**, **large cruises** to **historic ferry**, this definitive guide shows you how to compare, choose and most urgently secure your spot before it’s gone.

The best fireworks harbour tour and boat ride in Hong Kong

![2026 Lunar New Year Fireworks Harbour Tour - Aqua Luna Harbour Cruise](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/aqua-luna-fireworks-harbour-tour.jpg "2026 Lunar New Year Fireworks Harbour Tour - Aqua Luna Harbour Cruise")

Fireworks Harbour Tour

## When is Hong Kong Chinese New Year Fireworks display?

Every year on the 2nd day of Lunar New Year, Hong Kong will celebrate the New Year with thousands of pyrotechnic shells.

Hong Kong’s fireworks display is always one of the best in the world. All the fireworks will be shot from the barges and boats floating on [Victoria Harbour](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/victoria-harbor/ "What can you do on Victoria Harbour Hong Kong") right over the heads of people.

The Chinese New Year Fireworks display is always scheduled on the 2nd of the Lunar New Year. **For 2026, the fireworks show is scheduled to be at 8:00pm and lasts 23 minutes on the 18th of February 2026**. Tens of thousands of pyrotechnic shells will be discharged in the night sky from the barges and boats on Victoria Harbour.

Read more about [What to expect on 2026 Chinese New Year Fireworks display – programs, scenes, insider tips](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/hong-kong-chinese-new-year-fireworks/ "What to expect on 2026 Lunar New Year Fireworks display - where to watch, insider tips").

[Go Back to Top](#top)

## Fireworks harbour tour – Enjoy Chinese New Year Fireworks display on Victoria Harbour

Hong Kong’s fireworks are shot from the barges on Victoria Harbour in front of Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Center. Watching the fireworks on boat in the close vicinity would without a doubt bring a jaw-dropping visual impact.

![Watching fireworks display from Hong Kong Island](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/fireworks-from-hong-kong-island.jpg "Watching fireworks display from Hong Kong Island")

Fireworks shot from the barges

Hong Kong has major 7 harbour tour operators: **Aqua Luna, Dukling, Star Ferry, Watertours, Bauhinia Cruise, luxury yacht and the new Oriental Pearl Cruise**. Each year, they will offer special fireworks harbour tours to enhance the watching experience.

Several operators have already announced this year’s packages. Get yourself a ticket when the offer is still available.

[Go Back to Top](#top)

## Why watching fireworks on boat tour is the best way to see Hong Kong fireworks – benefits

You may wonder since there are so many free spots with good views, why should you book a fireworks boat tour? Choosing a boat tour over a land-based viewing spot offers a multitude of advantages.

### 1. Secure the best spots with no worries – avoid the massive crowds

The fireworks show is one of the most anticipated events for CNY celebration. Hundreds of thousands of people will gather along Victoria Harbour (especially those free spots). In order to get a good viewing location, you may have to get there hours in advance.

Waiting for hours is not the most devastated part. Imagine being squeezed by thousands of people in the crowds and you cannot get out for food, drink or toilets for hours. What’s more? After the fireworks show, the streets will be full of people that it may take another hour or longer to get out or to the nearest public transportation.

On a boat tour, you will only need to be there 15 – 20 minutes ahead of embarking. You have your own dedicated space to move, sit and enjoy in a relaxing environment. Some cruises have dinner included.

Watching from water is not just an activity; it’s the pinnacle of luxury, comfort and awe-inspiring perspective. Image the world-class skyline becomes the canvas for a spectacular fireworks display. While tens of thousands crowd the land-based viewing spots along the harbour, the most privileged view is undeniably from the water.

![Crowds watching fireworks along TST Waterfront](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/crowds-watching-fireworks-hong-kong.jpg "Crowds watching fireworks along TST Waterfront")

Crowds watching fireworks along TST Waterfront

### 2. Ultra-close watching distance and unobstructed 360-degree views

Needless to say that enjoying fireworks on the harbour cruise offers an ultra-close front seat. You will experience the jaw-dropping view that the fireworks blooms overhead.

Besides, you can escape the crowds and tall buildings and gain the completely clear, panoramic view of the sky, the skyline and the pyrotechnics.

### 3. Photo-perfect moments

Capture stunning, crowd-free photographs and videos. Th unique vantage point from the water allows for breathtaking shots of the fireworks bursting in front of the skyline.

### 4. Luxury amenities and atmosphere

Fireworks show stars at 8:00pm. Most fireworks boat tours are offering dinner/dinner buffet on board. Enjoy a welcome drink, a delicious buffet and a small open bar.

### 5. From budget options to the luxury tours

Not all the fireworks harbour tours are very expensive. There are several tour operators offering boat tour without meal on board with fair price (lower to around HK$ 360 per person – Sun Ferry). These offers are Coming Soon quickly. So secure your ticket ASAP.

[Go Back to Top](#top)

## How to choose harbour tours? Which boat ride is better? Which ones are still available?

Hong Kong offers a variety of boat tours to suit different preferences and budgets. All of the cruises/boats will get to the close vicinity to the fireworks display. All the tour time are between 2.5 hours – 3 hours. Most of them have complete / limited open-deck area for watching fireworks.

The major differences are the prices and with/without the meal on board. So which is the best is depending on your own preferences.

• If you would like to enjoy the atmosphere of heritage junk boat, then [Aqua Luna](#aqua_luna "Aqua Luna Harbour Cruise") and [Dukling](#dukling "Dukling Junk Boat Ride") are the options. Aqua Luna has two boats while Dukling has only one.

• If you prefer sharing the ride with less people, the [luxury yacht](#yacht "Yacht cruise on Victoria Harbour") is a good choice. Yacht is usually the most popular option that will be booked out soon. There are several yacht operators with / without meal on board.

* **If you would like to enjoy the atmosphere of heritage junk boat**, then [Aqua Luna](#aqua_luna "Aqua Luna Harbour Cruise") and [Dukling](#dukling "Dukling Junk Boat Ride") are the options. Aqua Luna has two boats while Dukling has only one.
* **If you prefer sharing the ride with less people**, the [luxury yacht](#yacht "Yacht cruise on Victoria Harbour") is a good choice. Yacht is usually the most popular option that will be booked out soon. There are several yacht operators with / without meal on board. The price ranges from HK$ 680 – HK$ 1,488.
* **If you don’t care about the vessel type or you travel on limited budget**, [Sun Ferry](#sun_ferry "Sun Ferry") (HK$ 320), [Watertours Boat Trip](#watertours "Watertours Cruise"), [Star Ferry](#star_ferry "Star Ferry Star Ferry Tour"), [Fortune Ferry](#fortune "Fortune Ferry boat tour") (HK$ 520) and [Water Taxi](#water_taxi "Water Taxi Harbour Tour") (HK$ 420) may be your right option. Their prices are be lower to HK$ 360.
* **If you have seasickness and would like to have more stable ride**, then [Star Ferry](#star_ferry "Star Ferry Water Tour") , [Bauhinia Cruise](#bauhinia "Bauhinia Harbour Cruise") and [Oriental Pearl](#oriental "Oriental Pearl Harbour Cruise") are larger vessels, which are more spacious. Bauhinia Cruise and Oriental Pearl also provide buffet on board, thus they will be a bit more expensive.

[Go Back to Top](#top)

## Types of fireworks cruises and boat tours for Chinese New Year Fireworks

Several major tour operators have already announced their offers for 2026 Chinese New Year Fireworks junk boat tour, yacht tour and large cruise tour . Note that the private yachts and the budget-friendly offers will be booked out soon. Act fast and get your ticket before tickets sell out.

### Aqua Luna Chinese junk harbour tour

For a quintessential Hong Kong experience, sail on a beautifully restored red-sail junk boat. Aqua Luna junk is operated by the Aqua Restaurant Group and is one of the last remaining traditional junks still in operation in Hong Kong.

Aqua Luna has two different junk boats. One is with red sail, which is older. The new one has a dark orange sail. For the Lunar New Year Fireworks harbour tour, both boats will be in service.

![Hong Kong Fireworks Harbour Tour on Aqua Luna junk boat](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/aqua-luna-junk-boat-tour-victoria-harbour-hk-2.jpg "Hong Kong Fireworks Harbour Tour on Aqua Luna junk boat")

Red-sail and orange-sail Aqua Luna junk boats

#### (Sold Out) 2026 Aqua Luna Chinese New Year Fireworks Boat Tour

Program:　2-hour night tour with pass around Canapés from HuTong & Aqua restaurant  
 Highlight:　Watch fireworks display; 2 glasses of sparkling wine, house wine, beer, soft drink or juice  
 Date:　February 18, 2026  
 Tour time:　 7:15pm – 9:15pm (Aqua Luna I)  
 Tour time:　 7:00pm – 9:00pm (Aqua Luna II)  
 Location:　7:00pm and 7:15pm embark at Shau Kei Wan Typhoon Shelter Pier  
 Price:　Ticket for adult is HK$ 1,598. The price for Child (4-11) is HK$ 888. Kids under 4 is free of charge.

Please refer to the official website of Aqua Luna ([https://www.aqualuna.com.hk/](https://aqualuna.com.hk/ "Aqua Luna")) for more information about the special day cruises as well as the exact sailing schedule and pick up times.

[Go Back to Top](#top)

## When is Hong Kong Chinese New Year Fireworks display?

Every year on the 2nd day of Lunar New Year, Hong Kong will celebrate the New Year with thousands of pyrotechnic shells.

Hong Kong’s fireworks display is always one of the best in the world. All the fireworks will be shot from the barges and boats floating on [Victoria Harbour](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/victoria-harbor/ "What can you do on Victoria Harbour Hong Kong") right over the heads of people.

The Chinese New Year Fireworks display is always scheduled on the 2nd of the Lunar New Year. **For 2026, the fireworks show is scheduled to be at 8:00pm and lasts 23 minutes on the 18th of February 2026**. Tens of thousands of pyrotechnic shells will be discharged in the night sky from the barges and boats on Victoria Harbour.

Read more about [What to expect on 2026 Chinese New Year Fireworks display – programs, scenes, insider tips](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/hong-kong-chinese-new-year-fireworks/ "What to expect on 2026 Lunar New Year Fireworks display - where to watch, insider tips").

## Why watching fireworks on boat tour is the best way to see Hong Kong fireworks – benefits

You may wonder since there are so many free spots with good views, why should you book a fireworks boat tour? Choosing a boat tour over a land-based viewing spot offers a multitude of advantages.

### 1. Secure the best spots with no worries – avoid the massive crowds

The fireworks show is one of the most anticipated events for CNY celebration. Hundreds of thousands of people will gather along Victoria Harbour (especially those free spots). In order to get a good viewing location, you may have to get there hours in advance.

Waiting for hours is not the most devastated part. Imagine being squeezed by thousands of people in the crowds and you cannot get out for food, drink or toilets for hours. What’s more? After the fireworks show, the streets will be full of people that it may take another hour or longer to get out or to the nearest public transportation.

On a boat tour, you will only need to be there 15 – 20 minutes ahead of embarking. You have your own dedicated space to move, sit and enjoy in a relaxing environment. Some cruises have dinner included.

Watching from water is not just an activity; it’s the pinnacle of luxury, comfort and awe-inspiring perspective. Image the world-class skyline becomes the canvas for a spectacular fireworks display. While tens of thousands crowd the land-based viewing spots along the harbour, the most privileged view is undeniably from the water.

![Crowds watching fireworks along TST Waterfront](https://www.nextstophongkong.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/crowds-watching-fireworks-hong-kong.jpg "Crowds watching fireworks along TST Waterfront")

Crowds watching fireworks along TST Waterfront

### 2. Ultra-close watching distance and unobstructed 360-degree views

Needless to say that enjoying fireworks on the harbour cruise offers an ultra-close front seat. You will experience the jaw-dropping view that the fireworks blooms overhead.

Besides, you can escape the crowds and tall buildings and gain the completely clear, panoramic view of the sky, the skyline and the pyrotechnics.

### 3. Photo-perfect moments

Capture stunning, crowd-free photographs and videos. Th unique vantage point from the water allows for breathtaking shots of the fireworks bursting in front of the skyline.

### 4. Luxury amenities and atmosphere

Fireworks show stars at 8:00pm. Most fireworks boat tours are offering dinner/dinner buffet on board. Enjoy a welcome drink, a delicious buffet and a small open bar.

### 5. From budget options to the luxury tours

Not all the fireworks harbour tours are very expensive. There are several tour operators offering boat tour without meal on board with fair price (lower to around HK$ 360 per person – Sun Ferry). These offers are Coming Soon quickly. So secure your ticket ASAP.

## How to choose harbour tours? Which boat ride is better? Which ones are still available?

Hong Kong offers a variety of boat tours to suit different preferences and budgets. All of the cruises/boats will get to the close vicinity to the fireworks display. All the tour time are between 2.5 hours – 3 hours. Most of them have complete / limited open-deck area for watching fireworks.

The major differences are the prices and with/without the meal on board. So which is the best is depending on your own preferences.

• If you would like to enjoy the atmosphere of heritage junk boat, then [Aqua Luna](#aqua_luna "Aqua Luna Harbour Cruise") and [Dukling](#dukling "Dukling Junk Boat Ride") are the options. Aqua Luna has two boats while Dukling has only one.

• If you prefer sharing the ride with less people, the [luxury yacht](#yacht "Yacht cruise on Victoria Harbour") is a good choice. Yacht is usually the most popular option that will be booked out soon. There are several yacht operators with / without meal on board.

### Hong Kong Harbour Tours – Dukling The Last Authentic Traditional Fishing Junk

Dukling was built in Macau in the 1950s and served as a fishing boat in Hong Kong till 1985. It was restored to her original glory and re-sails on Victoria Harbour since 2005. As an icon of Hong Kong, it is now “the only inheritant sailing junk in Hong Kong”. It represents Hong Kong’s glorious history as well as the impression of Hong Kong while Hong Kong was a fishing village.

## Planning Tips & Essential Advice

### Q: What should I know about traffic on the fireworks night?

**A:** As one the most popular events in Hong Kong, there will be road closure and traffic controls of the major streets near Victoria Harbour as well as the nearby subway exits. It is recommended to arrive earlier if you need to board a harbour tour. Otherwise you may have trouble reaching the embarkation location.

**Update: The police have announced that the roads in Tsim Sha Tsui and the West Kowloon Cultural District area will be closed in phases from around 5:00pm of Feb 18, 2026.**  
From 5:00pm: TST, TST Center  
From 5:30pm: Kowloon Park Drive Northbound, Entire Star Ferry Pier and Bus Terminus  
From 6:00pm: West Kowloon Cultural District

On Hong Kong Island, roads in Central, Wan Chai, and Eastern District will be closed in phases from around 3:00pm.  
From 3:00pm Golden Bauhinia Square  
From 6:00pm Central District, Wanchai District

For more detailed information of road control, please refer to government’s announcement: [Special Traffic and Transport Arrangements](https://www.td.gov.hk/en/traffic_notices/index_id_85311.html "Special Traffic and Transport Arrangements").

### Q: Why are these tours so much more expensive than regular harbour cruises?

**A:** The prices of the above tours are usually higher than their usual prices (almost 2-5 times). This is because these tours will be during the firework display. Therefore, if you are more interested in a general boat tour instead of a specific fireworks harbour tour, you can explore other options of [Hong Kong Victoria Harbour Tours](https://www.nextstophongkong.com//harbour-tours/ "Harbour Tours").

### Q: What if I miss the chance to get a ticket this time?

**A:** Don’t be disappointed! Hong Kong usually hosts major fireworks displays on other holidays, most notably **National Day (October 1st)** and **New Year’s Eve (December 31st)**. If you are visiting Hong Kong during those periods, you can consider booking a fireworks harbour tour for those dates instead.

## Major fireworks events in Hong Kong

Every year there are 3 annual fireworks events in Hong Kong.

• Chinese New Year Fireworks (Typically in January/February): A vibrant display celebrating the Lunar New Year;

• National Day Fireworks (October 1st): a major show commemorating the founding of the People’s Republic of China;

• New Year’s Eve Count Down Fireworks (December 31st): a giant party welcoming new year;

### Very valuable boat buffet

Apr 2026 • Couples

Very valuable boat buffet! There are two rounds per day, all boarding at Northern Harbor, with the first row departing at 5:15 p.m. and the second row departing at 7:20 p.m. On the boat at 5:15 p.m., I bought tickets at the travel agency for just $203 per person. The selection and level of buffet dishes on board can not be compared to the hotel buffet, but it is also abundant, there are more than ten hot dishes, salads, desserts, coffee and tea, and salad for own charge. There are live performances of the band on board, and you can also see the scenery on the deck on both sides of Wi-Hong, making the entire approximately two-hour cruise very enjoyable!

About

“Harbour Cruise – Bauhinia”, operated by the Hong Kong Ferry Group (HKEX number:0050), is one of the leading cruise service providers in Hong Kong. As the only Hong Kong company that owns cruise vessels with marine restaurant license, liquor license and entertainment license, it has been as popular as ever to overseas tourists and local residents. Since 1998, vehicular ferries have been converted to become bright and colorful cruise ships of Harbour Cruise – Bauhinia providing cruise and dining services in Hong Kong water. Harbour Cruise – Bauhinia boasts four triple-deck, multi-functional vessels, namely Man On, Man Lok, Man Kim and Man Foo. With a fine touch of elegance, each can accommodate over 300 guests on any occasions. On the cruise, you can enjoy the breathtaking sunset view, the spectacular world-renowned lasers show “A Symphony of Lights”, gorgeous views of both sides of Victoria Harbour and fabulous architectures on its shore, and to perceive the sensation of a luxurious world of taste. To light up your day, you can also interact with the inspiring and energetic city of Hong Kong on our 360 degrees open deck. Harbour Cruise - Bauhinia welcomes you on board to experience special moments for you and your treasured guests!

### [We were glad we included the Bauhinia Dinner Cruise in our Hong Kong travel plans.](https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g294217-d2489278-r752521025-Harbour_Cruise_Bauhinia-Hong_Kong.html)

Dec 2019

My wife and I were part of an afternoon and evening Grayline HK tour of Hong Kong Island on 29 December 2019 which culminated in a Harbour Dinner Cruise Bauhinia experience incorporating the renowned daily Symphony of Lights experience. 

The afternoon part of our tour included a funicular railway ride by tram to Victoria Peak where the rain that day totally obliterated the spectacular views on offer, an enjoyable visit to the Aberdeen fishing village and an interesting visit to Tai Kwun – the Centre for Heritage and Arts, the former police station complex of Hong Kong. The rain all afternoon, on and off, made for a frustrating tour and so we were looking forward to our Bauhinia Dinner Cruise with great anticipation. 

Well I have to say that the Cruise was very enjoyable despite having to view the Symphony of Light Show on the Harbour in the rain. Our ship was the Man Lok, one of four converted vehicular ferries in the Bauhinia fleet. The best vantage points were outside on the deck of the ship so trying to keep the cameras dry was a challenge in itself. The Light Show was a little disappointing and certainly not as spectacular as we had hoped. Maybe the rain had something to do with that. Nevertheless it was still an enjoyable experience and I managed to get some interesting memento photos. 

I agree with other reviewers that it is unfortunate that Dinner was taking place while the Light Show was happening and it would be better if the staff waited until the show was over before patrons could commence the buffet. I chose to have a later meal because I did not want to miss the Light show. 

There were only a handful of guests on the cruise on the night we were there so a lot of atmosphere was missing to an extent. However we did enjoy a splendid International buffet meal, with a wide range of soups, meats, seafood and salads and a great selection of desserts. There was one humorous moment when the main waiter on our table tried to take my plate away from me before I had finished my meal. When he realised his error he acknowledged this with a nice smile showing that wait staff like all of us are only human. 

We loved the entertainment provided by a small group of young entertainers and we were kept entertained for most of the night with a wide range of songs of all genres. They even took requests from the audience to show their versatility.

Hong Kong is blessed with a wonderful harbour which was showcased splendidly with the many night lights on this cruise despite the ordinary weather. 

The wait staff members on the ship were friendly and efficient and looked after us throughout the evening. 

This was a great evening to experience a pleasing aspect of night time Hong Kong. We were glad we chose to include the Bauhinia Dinner Cruise in our Hong Kong travel plans.

[Top](http://www.chinatourguide.com/hongkong/victoria_harbour_cruise.html#)

Victoria Harbour

![Image 40: Victoria Harbour and Cruise](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/victoria_harbour_and_cruise.jpg)Hong Kong, with its dazzling **Firework Celebration around Victoria Harbour**, is ranked 1 of _**World's 10 Top Destinations in which to welcome 2013**_ by CNN.

**Victoria Harbour**, one of the world's busiest and most impressive natural harbours, is also renowned for its stunning panoramic night view and skyline of the cosmopolitan Hong Kong city. Take a Victoria Harbour Cruise around would surely be a worthwhile choice for justifying your journey.

 The fantastic views of Victoria Harbour particularly lie in the direction towards Hong Kong Island where the skyline of skyscrapers is superimposed over the ridges behind. The best places to catch the most charming Harbour into your sight are: at the Victoria Tower on the Victoria Peak, from the piazza at the Culture Centre, from the promenade of Tsim Sha Tsui on the Kowloon side, or ride on a cruise around the Harbour.

During some holidays like Chinese New Year or Christmas, Victoria Harbour will display more liveliness and vitality. As the natural centre of the territory, Victoria Harbour has played host to many major public shows including the annual fireworks. At night there is even a grand show named "A Symphony of Lights", which was recorded as "The World's Largest Permanent Light and Sound Show" by Guinness World Records in 2005.

[![Image 41: Victoria Harbour](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/harbour_strolling_at_dusk.jpg)](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/harbour_strolling_at_dusk.jpg)[![Image 42: Hong Kong Night](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/hrc_hong_kong_night_view_1.jpg)](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/hrc_hong_kong_night_view_1.jpg)
Strolling along the Harbour at Dusk Vibrant Hong Kong Viewed from Victoria Harbour

![Image 43: Victoria Harbour Cruise - Bauhinia](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/victoria_harbour_cruise_bauhinia.jpg)Harbour Cruise - Bauhinia

 Operated by the Hong Kong Ferry Group, “Harbour Cruise – Bauhinia” is a unique sightseeing and dining cruise. Taking a cruise tour around Victoria Harbour, you can feast your eyes on the glory sunset, the amazing lasers show “A Symphony of Lights,” fabulous cityscape on both sides of the Harbour.

 The cruise will sail past Central, Admiralty, Wanchai, Causeway Bay, Eastern Hong Kong Island, Eastern Kowloon, and Tsim Sha Tsui.

Dining Room Onboard Dancing Hall Onboard

"A Symphony of Lights" Show

Awarded as **The World's Largest Permanent Light and Sound Show** by Guinness World Records in 2005, "**A Symphony of Lights**" of Victoria Harbour is a synchronized multimedia spectacle organized by the Hong Kong Tourism Board, combining interactive lights of 44 key buildings on both Hong Kong Island and Kowloon with musical effects, displaying the vitality and glory of Hong Kong. The show is organized by Hong Kong Tourism Board and performed every night with good weather at 20:00pm and lasts for about 14 minutes.

[![Image 48: A Symphony of Lights Show](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/Hong_Kong_night_view_e.jpg)](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/Hong_Kong_night_view_e.jpg)[![Image 49: Hong Kong Firework](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/hongkong_firework.jpg)](http://www.chinatourguide.com/china_photos/Hongkong/Attractions/hongkong_firework.jpg)
"A Symphony of Lights" Show Light Show & Firework Brighten the City

Themes

The Show is made up of five major themes – _**Awakening, Energy, Heritage, Partnership and Celebration**_. It guides spectators to an extraordinary journey admiring and celebrating the energy, spirit and diversity of Hong Kong.

**>> Awakening**

Laser lights flash, gradually illuminating the participating buildings. The colored light beams like a rainbow, dancing in the vibrant night. This scene symbolizes the birth and growth of Hong Kong.

**>> Energy**

Color patterns rise up and lasers and searchlights sweep energetically across the night sky, displaying the vibrant energy of Hong Kong.

**>> Heritage**

Traditional lucky red and gold colors are showed across buildings on both sides of the Harbour, along with the introduction of Chinese traditional music, symbolizing Hong Kong’s colorful heritage and rich cultural traditions.

**>> Partnership**

Laser beams and searchlights display across the Harbour, representing an illuminated connection with the opposite side and symbolizing a greater and unified partnership.

**>> Celebration**

With the rhythm beats high, the colorful lights and beams swirl and dance enthusiastically across the Harbour, representing an even brighter future for this cosmopolitan Hong Kong.

Vantage Points

The best viewing locations of this nightly spectacle are:

>> Along the Tsim Sha Tsui waterfront between the Avenue of Stars and the Hong Kong Cultural Centre

>> Promenade at Golden Bauhinia Square in Wan Chai

>> From sightseeing ferries in the Victoria Harbour

Firework Displays

On some special occasions, the Hong Kong Tourism Board may arrange to enhance the festive ambience through launching firework displays in parallel to "A Symphony of Lights".

## At a glance

Non-refundable

Duration varies

Enjoy a memorable evening on the Harbour Cruise - Bauhinia with a dinner buffet, live band performance, and stunning views of Victoria Harbour. This cruise offers a unique sightseeing experience in Hong Kong, combining delicious food with vibrant entertainment on the water.

*   The dinner buffet features a variety of dishes for guests to enjoy while cruising Victoria Harbour.
*   A live band performs onboard, creating a lively atmosphere throughout the journey.
*   The cruise route allows for panoramic sightseeing of both sides of Victoria Harbour.
*   Each guest will be assigned seating by the staff, and tables are shared with other participants.

results.title: Harbour Cruise Bauhinia
results.content:
Image 46Image 47

Set sail with Harbour Cruise Bauhinia for dinner cruises that combine scenic splendour of Victoria Harbour with culinary delights. As the vessel navigates the city’s panorama, indulge in a lavish buffet featuring Eastern and Western dishes.

While enjoying the food, you can also step out onto the open deck to enjoy the fresh sea breeze, and immerse yourself in the enchanting scenery along both sides of the harbour.

Looking for an even more mesmerising experience? Consider the ‘A Symphony of Lights’ dinner buffet, where you can enjoy Hong Kong’s famous light show alongside live music performances on board, revelling in a vibrant atmosphere that adds extra sparkle to your evening.

Information on this page is subject to change without advance notice.

![Image 46](https://www.discoverhongkong.com/etc.clientlibs/dhk-cloud/clientlibs/clientlib-site/resources/images/icons/arrow.png)![Image 47](https://www.discoverhongkong.com/etc.clientlibs/dhk-cloud/clientlibs/clientlib-site/resources/images/icons/arrow.png)

Set sail with Harbour Cruise Bauhinia for dinner cruises that combine scenic splendour of Victoria Harbour with culinary delights. As the vessel navigates the city’s panorama, indulge in a lavish buffet featuring Eastern and Western dishes.

While enjoying the food, you can also step out onto the open deck to enjoy the fresh sea breeze, and immerse yourself in the enchanting scenery along both sides of the harbour.

Looking for an even more mesmerising experience? Consider the ‘A Symphony of Lights’ dinner buffet, where you can enjoy Hong Kong’s famous light show alongside live music performances on board, revelling in a vibrant atmosphere that adds extra sparkle to your evening.
